title: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS): Is This AI Power Play Worth the Risk Despite Declining Revenue?
text: Navitas Semiconductor faces a fundamental disconnect between its market valuation and operational performance. The company trades at approximately $3.26 billion while generating minimal revenue, betting everything on future AI infrastructure adoption. 2025 Annual Revenue was $45.9 million (down 45% from $83.3M in 2024), Q4 2025 Revenue was $7.3 million, Q1 2026 Guidance was $8.0M–$8.5M, and Market Cap was approximately $3.26 billion. This represents an extreme valuation multiple relative to current operations. Navitas specializes in gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductors. The company targets AI data centers, electric vehicles, and industrial applications, positioning these advanced materials as superior to conventional silicon technology. Management raised $194.6 million in 2025, primarily through $200 million in equity offerings, extending operational runway through shareholder dilution. Simultaneously, the company implemented a 19% workforce reduction to concentrate resources on priority segments. The article characterizes NVTS as more appropriately a speculative growth opportunity rather than an established operational performer, contingent on materializing design wins in AI power applications. The risk-reward profile requires exceptional execution and timing to justify current valuations.
